user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single submitted image clearly shows the rear bumper area of a car, which is the location specified in the chat transcript for the claimed dent.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims there is a dent on the back of the car/rear bumper area. Image img_1 clearly shows visible deformation and damage (dents) to the rear bumper cover and surrounding body panels.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the front bumper area of a car, which is the claimed location for the scratch.
claim_status_justification: Both submitted images (img_1 and img_2) show the front bumper area. Image img_1 provides a close-up view of the damage, showing visible surface marks consistent with a scratch on the bumper.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the claimed object (windshield) and the alleged damage (cracks). The claim is specific to the front glass.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a crack from a small stone impact on the windshield. Image img_1 clearly shows multiple spiderweb cracks across the front glass, supporting the claim of damage.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single submitted image clearly shows the side mirror area of a car, which is the part claimed to be damaged.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows visible damage (a crack/break) on the side mirror assembly, consistent with the customer's claim that the mirror was damaged.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the rear bumper area of a vehicle, which is the part claimed to be damaged.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims bumper damage from being tapped from behind. Image img_2 clearly shows visible deformation and scuffing on the rear bumper area, supporting a claim of impact damage.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The customer claims a crack on the headlight. While the submitted image shows the car's front area, the specific headlight in question is not clearly visible or inspected for cracks. The angle and lighting make it impossible to confirm damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the headlight is cracked, but the provided image (img_1) does not offer a clear, close-up view of the headlight to confirm any crack or damage. The angle and lighting are insufficient for proper inspection.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show a car (img_1) and provide a close-up view of the door panel area where the customer claims damage (img_2). The visible dent on the door is clear enough for assessment.
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 clearly shows a noticeable indentation/dent on the side of the car's door panel, consistent with the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the hood of a car, allowing for inspection of surface marks as claimed.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a scratch on the hood. Image img_1 clearly shows visible surface scratches and marks across the top panel of the hood.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the laptop screen with visible cracks, directly addressing the customer's claim about display glass damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer claimed the display glass has a crack, and image img_1 clearly shows multiple visible cracks across the screen surface.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the laptop's hinge area and body, allowing for inspection of the claimed damage (broken/wobbling hinge).
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the hinge area is broken and wobbles. Image img_1 shows a clear gap/damage along the seam where the screen meets the body, consistent with a broken or damaged hinge mechanism. Image img_2 provides context of the overall laptop condition.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the laptop keyboard area, which is the part claimed to be affected by water damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer reported a spill over the keys leaving a stain and sticky feeling. Image img_1 clearly shows visible liquid/stain residue on the keyboard area, supporting the claim of water damage.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The customer claims a dent on one corner of the laptop. While both images show the laptop body, neither image clearly shows any visible dent or damage to the corners as claimed.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a dent on one corner. Image img_1 shows the top surface, and image img_2 shows an edge/side view. Neither image provides clear visual evidence of a dent on any visible corner.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the laptop screen with visible damage consistent with a shattered display.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the screen is shattered, and image img_1 clearly shows multiple visible cracks across the laptop screen.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the trackpad area of the laptop, which is the part claimed to be damaged.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a visible indentation or dent on the trackpad area, consistent with physical damage from hitting an edge.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the package corner area where damage is claimed.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims one corner was crushed, and img_1 shows visible crushing/denting on the top-front corner of the package.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the package exterior, allowing for inspection of torn packaging.
claim_status_justification: The customer specifically requested a review of torn packaging. Image img_1 clearly shows significant tearing and damage along the top/side seam of the package, supporting the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single submitted image clearly shows a brown cardboard package with visible staining on its surface, directly addressing the customer's claim of water damage/stains.
claim_status_justification: The image (img_1) clearly shows a dark, wet-looking stain on the side of the package, consistent with the customer's description of water damage upon arrival.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The claim is about missing contents ('contents'), which requires a clear view of the opened package and the empty space inside. While img_1 shows an open box, it only provides a general overview of the packaging material/void fill and does not clearly demonstrate that the intended product area is empty or that the item was never present.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the contents are missing. While img_1 shows an open box, it does not provide sufficient visual evidence to confirm that a specific item is missing or that the package was opened improperly. The images only show void fill/packing material.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single submitted image clearly shows the exterior of a package box, allowing for inspection of the claimed damage (crushing).
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the outside box was badly crushed. Image img_1 shows visible signs of crushing and deformation on the package exterior.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the package exterior and the claimed damage (torn seal/open box).
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the delivery box arrived opened and that the seal area looked torn. Image img_1 clearly shows a tear/opening in the tape/seal on the side of the package, supporting the claim.
